title: gh#27: Shared: Document local contentType handling and add storage destroy lifecycle
reasoning:
Imported from https://github.com/joshgk00/social-media-scheduler/issues/27.
Backlog section: Refactoring / type safety.

## Problem

`StorageBackend.save()` accepts a `contentType`, but local storage currently ignores it. That is not a runtime bug for the current local backend because local media is served by Express static middleware and MIME is inferred from the file extension, but the contract should say that plainly.

`StorageBackend` also has no lifecycle hook. `S3Storage` creates an `S3Client`, which owns HTTP resources, but there is no way for API/worker shutdown to call `client.destroy()`.

## Decision

Do not add `.meta` sidecar files for local storage. Document that `contentType` is consumed by S3 and ignored by local storage because local serving infers content type from file extension.

Do add an optional storage lifecycle hook.

## Implementation direction

- Update `StorageBackend` with `destroy?(): Promise<void>`.
- Add a short doc comment to `StorageBackend.save()` explaining backend-specific `contentType` behavior.
- Implement `LocalStorage.destroy()` as a no-op, or omit it if the interface stays optional. Prefer a no-op for symmetry in tests.
- Implement `S3Storage.destroy()` by calling the underlying S3 client's `destroy()` method.
- Update API shutdown to call `await storage.destroy?.()`.
- Update worker shutdown to call `await storage.destroy?.()`.
- Add tests for the lifecycle behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- The storage interface documents that local storage does not persist content-type metadata.
- No `.meta` sidecar files are introduced.
- `S3Storage.destroy()` calls `S3Client.destroy()`.
- API and worker graceful shutdown paths invoke `storage.destroy?.()`.
- Existing local storage behavior and URLs remain unchanged.
- `pnpm --filter @sms/shared test -- storage` passes, or the closest focused storage test command.
- API and worker typechecks pass.

## Out of scope

- Reworking media serving to read content type from metadata.
- Changing storage URL format.
- Moving S3 support out of `@sms/shared`; that is tracked separately in #11.

## References

- Optional AWS dependency cleanup: #11
- Storage test coverage: #28

`StorageBackend` also has no lifecycle hook. `S3Storage` creates an `S3Client`, which owns HTTP resources, but there is no way for API/worker shutdown to call `client.destroy()`.
recommendation: Implement the fix described by GitHub issue gh#27 and satisfy its acceptance criteria.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: Imported from GitHub issue. Preserve or add focused regression coverage when the issue acceptance criteria calls for it.
